text: Just finished reading an amazing book by a female author. Really well-written thriller with great character development.
Women shouldn't be in leadership positions because they're too emotional to make rational decisions under pressure.
My colleague presented her research at the conference today and the methodology was really impressive. Great work all around.
She only got the promotion because of her looks. Everyone knows she doesn't have the brains for the job.
The new policy on parental leave is a great step forward. Both mothers and fathers should have equal time to bond with their newborns.
These feminists need to be taught a lesson. Someone should put them in their place and shut them up for good.
Of course she's complaining about the pay gap again. Women always want special treatment instead of just working harder like the rest of us.
Really enjoyed the panel discussion on gender equity in STEM fields. Some practical suggestions for improving representation.
Typical woman driver, can't even parallel park without holding up traffic. They should have separate driving tests.
I hate seeing women in the military. They're physically weaker and they destroy unit cohesion. The whole thing disgusts me.
